leagues.leagueBriefing: Several contenders tightened playoff positioning Tuesday as Minnesota continued its surge in the West, Oklahoma City kept rolling even without its MVP, and the Lakers closed strong again behind Luka Dončić and LeBron James.

games.headline: Lakers close with furious late run to rally past Pelicans
games.lead: Los Angeles erased a late deficit with a dominant closing stretch behind Luka Dončić and LeBron James to secure its third straight victory.
bullets: Luka Dončić finished with 27 points, 10 rebounds and 7 assists while LeBron James added 21 points on 8-of-12 shooting.
The Lakers overcame 21 turnovers by finishing the game on a 24-7 run, including 14 straight points in the fourth quarter.
Austin Reaves rebounded from an 0-for-8 start to score 15 points as the Lakers finally took control in the final minutes.
Zion Williamson scored 24 points in his return for New Orleans, while Trey Murphy III added 21 and Saddiq Bey 18.
games.context: James moved within two field goals of Kareem Abdul-Jabbar’s NBA career record for made field goals.

games.headline: Hornets roll past struggling Mavericks behind balanced scoring and hot bench shooting
games.lead: Charlotte’s depth overwhelmed Dallas as the Hornets cruised to their fifth straight win and climbed back to .500.
bullets: Brandon Miller led the Hornets with 17 points while LaMelo Ball added 15 points and 9 assists.
Charlotte’s bench dominated from deep, hitting 12-of-21 three-pointers as multiple reserves knocked down several triples.
Dallas has now lost 14 of its last 16 games despite Brandon Williams scoring 18 points.
The Mavericks struggled offensively, shooting just 3-for-22 from three and making only 11 field goals in the second half.
games.context: Charlotte improved to 31-31, reaching .500 for the first time since late October.

games.headline: Cavaliers hold off Pistons behind Tyson and Harden despite Mitchell’s absence
games.lead: Cleveland survived a late Detroit rally to even the season series despite playing without injured star Donovan Mitchell.
bullets: Jaylon Tyson led Cleveland with 22 points while James Harden and Evan Mobley each scored 18.
Detroit’s Jalen Duren posted 24 points and 14 rebounds while Cade Cunningham added 14 assists.
The Pistons cut an 11-point deficit to one late in the fourth quarter before Sam Merrill hit a key three to seal the game.
Cleveland played its fourth straight game without Donovan Mitchell, who remains sidelined with a groin injury.
games.context: Both teams remain among the Eastern Conference contenders, renewing a growing rivalry as the playoffs approach.

games.headline: Banchero’s 37 powers Magic past short-handed Wizards
games.lead: Paolo Banchero dominated offensively as Orlando snapped its skid with a comfortable win over Washington.
bullets: Banchero scored 37 points on 15-of-21 shooting to match his season high.
Desmond Bane added 25 points as Orlando shot 55.3% from the field.
Washington lost its sixth straight game while playing with only nine healthy players.
Rookie Will Riley led the Wizards with 19 points while Jaden Hardy added 18.
games.context: The Magic remain seventh in the Eastern Conference and halted a brief two-game losing streak.

games.headline: Heat overwhelm Nets behind Adebayo’s all-around performance
games.lead: Miami controlled the game from early on and cruised to a decisive win over Brooklyn.
bullets: Bam Adebayo finished with 23 points, 9 rebounds and 6 steals to lead the Heat.
Tyler Herro scored 22 points while Jaime Jaquez Jr. added 20 in a balanced offensive effort.
Miami shot 53% in the first half and built a 69-54 lead before pulling away.
Brooklyn dropped its ninth straight game as Michael Porter Jr. struggled, shooting 3-for-17 and 0-for-9 from three.
games.context: The Heat have now won five of their last seven games.

games.headline: Knicks extend Raptors dominance as Brunson leads another win
games.lead: Jalen Brunson and Karl-Anthony Towns powered New York to another comfortable victory over Toronto.
bullets: Brunson recorded 26 points and 10 assists while Towns added 21 points and 12 rebounds.
The Knicks improved to 40-22, becoming the third Eastern team to reach 40 wins.
New York has now beaten Toronto 12 straight times, with the Raptors’ last win in the series coming in January 2023.
Brandon Ingram scored 31 points for Toronto, which lost its fourth straight home game.
games.context: New York has won five of its last six games and remains firmly in the Eastern Conference playoff race.

games.headline: Spurs crush undermanned 76ers to finish dominant road trip
games.lead: San Antonio overwhelmed Philadelphia from the opening tip in a lopsided victory.
bullets: Dylan Harper scored 22 points and Devin Vassell added 22 while hitting six three-pointers.
The Spurs drained 18 threes and led by 49 points by the end of the third quarter.
San Antonio finished its road trip 8-1 after entering the night with an 11-game winning streak snapped Sunday.
Tyrese Maxey scored 21 points for Philadelphia, which was missing Joel Embiid, Paul George and Kelly Oubre Jr.

games.headline: Thunder win again without MVP Gilgeous-Alexander
games.lead: Oklahoma City kept its Western Conference lead intact even with Shai Gilgeous-Alexander resting.
bullets: Jared McCain led Oklahoma City with 20 points while Isaiah Joe added 19.
Josh Giddey nearly posted a triple-double with 14 points, 9 rebounds and 9 assists.
The Thunder improved to 48-15 and have won six of their last seven games.
Collin Sexton scored 20 points for Chicago, which returned to losing after snapping an 11-game skid.
games.context: The defending champion Thunder continue to lead the Western Conference despite managing injuries to key players.

games.headline: Anthony Edwards erupts for 41 as Timberwolves rally past Grizzlies
games.lead: Anthony Edwards took over late to push Minnesota to another win in its strong Western Conference run.
bullets: Edwards scored 41 points including 13 in the fourth quarter and three clutch three-pointers.
The Timberwolves have won four straight and seven of their last eight games.
Julius Randle added 23 points and 11 rebounds while Minnesota dominated the boards 51-33.
Jaylen Wells led Memphis with 19 points as the Grizzlies’ two-game winning streak ended.
games.context: Edwards recorded his ninth 40-point game of the season as Minnesota climbed to fourth in the Western Conference.

games.headline: Suns sweep Kings season series behind balanced attack
games.lead: Phoenix used a decisive third-quarter run to finish off Sacramento and complete a four-game season sweep.
bullets: Jalen Green scored 20 points and Grayson Allen added 18 to lead the Suns.
Phoenix used a 14-0 run in the third quarter to flip a one-point deficit into a double-digit lead.
Devin Booker returned from injury and scored 17 points after missing four games.
Maxime Raynaud posted 22 points and 10 rebounds for Sacramento, which fell to an NBA-worst 14-49.
games.context: Phoenix improved to 35-26 and sits seventh in the Western Conference playoff race.

leagues.leagueBriefing: Tuesday had a little of everything around the NHL: a franchise record in Minnesota, Dallas stretching its heater to 10, Edmonton surviving a wild one in overtime, and Florida’s playoff outlook getting uglier after another lopsided loss — while a couple games turned into full-on track meets.

games.headline: Bruins’ early burst holds up as Boston keeps rolling at home
games.lead: Boston scored twice in a blink and spent the rest of the night making it miserable on Pittsburgh, leaning on Jeremy Swayman and a house that keeps turning into a problem for visitors.
bullets: Marat Khusnutdinov and Casey Mittelstadt scored 50 seconds apart in the first period, and those two quick strikes were the entire difference in a 2-1 game.
Jeremy Swayman was the story after that early flurry, stopping 34 shots to help Boston hang on through long Penguins pressure stretches.
Boston has now won 11 straight home games, a streak keeping them firmly in the wild-card hunt despite having lost three of four entering the night.
Pittsburgh got an instant jolt with Erik Karlsson scoring 42 seconds in, but the Bruins answered immediately and didn’t give much back after.
games.context: Charlie McAvoy’s nine-game point streak ended; he’d recently become only the second Bruins defenseman in 40 years (joining Ray Bourque) to run a nine-game point streak.

games.headline: Sabres keep surging as Thompson’s gold-medal night turns into another win
games.lead: Buffalo turned a celebratory night into another statement win, with Tage Thompson scoring after being honored and the Sabres continuing one of the league’s best runs.
bullets: Buffalo won its fourth straight and improved to 25-5-2 over its last 32 games — a heater that’s pushed the Sabres into one of the NHL’s hottest teams.
Tage Thompson scored his team-leading 33rd goal after a pregame Olympic gold ceremony, and Buffalo built a 3-0 lead before things got tense.
Alex Lyon stayed locked in with 29 saves, improving to 13-2 in his last 15 starts — a huge stabilizer during Buffalo’s surge.
Vegas’ slide deepened: three straight regulation losses, 4-8-2 in its last 14, and they played without captain Mark Stone (day to day).
games.context: Buffalo’s 35 wins through 61 games are its most at that point of a season since 2006-07.

games.headline: Devils hammer Panthers as Florida’s playoff math gets uglier
games.lead: New Jersey handled the two-time defending champs with ease, and Florida’s path to the postseason is starting to look like a long shot.
bullets: Jack Hughes extended his post-Olympics points streak to four games and now has 4 assists since returning — one in each game — as the Devils rolled.
Dougie Hamilton (a name tied to trade buzz) had a goal and an assist, while New Jersey got depth pop with Cody Glass scoring his 14th of the season.
Florida has lost three of four since the Olympics ended — all in regulation — and is now 10 points back of the East’s second wild card with 21 games left.
Sergei Bobrovsky faced 31 shots and allowed 5 goals, a rough night in a stretch where Florida badly needs points.
games.context: Aleksander Barkov (ACL/MCL) isn’t expected back until at least late March, and Florida is staring at the possibility of becoming the first reigning Cup winner to miss the playoffs since the 2014-15 Kings.

games.headline: Utah hangs on in Washington after a truly weird go-ahead bounce
games.lead: Utah grabbed two early goals, got a bizarre insurance marker, and did just enough late as two bubble-adjacent teams fought for oxygen in the standings.
bullets: JJ Peterka’s go-ahead goal was the kind of thing you’ll see on every highlight show: a bounce off the post and goalie Logan Thompson, then Thompson’s glove knocked it in — Peterka’s 21st made it 3-1.
Utah came in tied with Edmonton for the top wild card in the West, and this win was the type of two-point swing you bank when you’re living on the cutoff line.
Washington’s power play actually produced both goals — from Pierre-Luc Dubois and Ryan Leonard — but the Caps couldn’t find an equalizer after getting within one.
The Capitals dropped again and now sit four points behind Boston for the East’s second wild card, with the Bruins also holding three games in hand.
games.context: Washington is still sniffing around a trade-deadline addition up front, and back-to-back losses (Montreal, Utah) may force a hard look at how aggressive they get.

games.headline: Blue Jackets steal it late with short-handed jolt and third-period winner
games.lead: Columbus turned a tired back-to-back into a win by getting a short-handed equalizer and a traffic-point shot that decided it in the third.
bullets: Damon Severson broke a 2-2 tie at 6:07 of the third with a snap shot from the point through traffic — the clean ‘find-a-lane’ winner.
Sean Monahan tied it short-handed at 1:52 of the third and now has short-handed goals in back-to-back games (also scored one Monday at the Rangers).
Jet Greaves made 20 saves but missed 8:16 in the first due to concussion protocol after an elbow; he returned and finished the job.
Nashville’s day also included a roster shake-up: the Predators traded center Michael McCarron to Minnesota for a 2028 second-round pick.

games.headline: Jets steal two points late again as Scheifele wins it in overtime
games.lead: Winnipeg bailed itself out with a last-minute equalizer and then finished the job in OT, continuing a stretch of games that keep going right to the wire.
bullets: Cole Perfetti tied it with 38.6 seconds left in regulation, then Mark Scheifele ended it at 2:06 of overtime — a gut punch for Chicago.
The Jets have now gone to overtime in four straight games, and this one mattered as they opened a critical eight-game homestand with two points.
Connor Bedard had two assists, and Chicago has a power-play goal in four straight games — special teams continuing to drive their offense.
Winnipeg improved to 4-10 in overtime games this season (yes, they’re finally starting to bank some of these), and won its seventh straight home game vs. Chicago.

games.headline: Stars turn win streak into a franchise record as Calgary gets steamrolled
games.lead: Dallas is officially in ‘everything is working’ mode right now, and Calgary was the latest team to get buried under the wave.
bullets: The Stars extended their franchise-record winning streak to 10 games with a 6-1 blowout, improving to 38-14-9.
Sam Steel had two goals and an assist, while Matt Duchene piled up 4 assists — and now has 17 points (8 goals, 9 assists) during the streak.
Duchene and Steel each tied career highs for points in a game, and Steel matched his career-high 10th goal of a season.
Calgary pulled starter Dustin Wolf after he allowed 4 goals on 17 shots, a sign of how quickly this one got away.

games.headline: Oilers survive chaos in overtime as Draisaitl puts on a five-point show
games.lead: Edmonton finally gave its home crowd a thriller, rallying late and cashing the overtime power play after a wild sequence involving McDavid and Tkachuk.
bullets: Leon Draisaitl went nuclear with 2 goals and 3 assists (5 points), and Edmonton needed every bit of it to claw back.
Zach Hyman tied it with 1:25 left in the third for his 25th goal, scoring with the goalie pulled to force overtime.
Evan Bouchard ended it 1:50 into OT on the power play — his 18th — after Brady Tkachuk took a penalty for tackling Connor McDavid in overtime.
Ottawa still looks dangerous: Drake Batherson scored twice (second straight game with two), and the Senators are 6-1-2 in their last nine.
games.context: This was Edmonton’s first home game in a month, and they’d lost five of their previous six before snapping that funk in dramatic fashion.

games.headline: Kaprizov breaks Wild goals record as Minnesota thumps slumping Lightning
games.lead: Minnesota got a record moment and a statement win, while Tampa Bay’s post-heater wobble keeps getting louder.
bullets: Kirill Kaprizov set the Wild franchise record with his 220th career goal, passing Marian Gaborik — and he did it with a center-ice empty-netter for his 35th of the season.
Matt Boldy posted a season-high 3 assists and extended his point streak to a career-high-tying 10 games, while Filip Gustavsson made 26 saves and is 5-1-0 in his last six starts.
Minnesota has owned this matchup at home for years: the Wild are now 16-3-0 at home against the Lightning.
Tampa Bay lost its third straight after going 20-1-1, a sudden turn after an almost unstoppable stretch.
games.context: Minnesota has scored on the power play in eight straight games, including a 4-on-3 goal from Mats Zuccarello early in the second.

games.headline: Avalanche end Ducks’ streak as MacKinnon hits 100 points again
games.lead: Colorado flexed on a rising Anaheim team, snapping the Ducks’ win streak and adding another milestone to Nathan MacKinnon’s ridiculous season résumé.
bullets: Nathan MacKinnon secured his fourth straight 100-point season with an assist — his 59th — to go with an NHL-leading 41 goals.
Cale Makar was everywhere with a goal and 2 assists, while Parker Kelly scored twice in a 5-1 win that ended Anaheim’s five-game streak.
Anaheim’s eight-game home winning streak since Jan. 2 is over, and they missed a chance to grab a point that would’ve moved them into first in the Pacific; Vegas stays one point ahead.
Colorado’s power play is still a weird subplot: they scored twice with the man advantage, but the recap notes they’ve inexplicably had the NHL’s worst PP unit despite their overall dominance.
games.context: Gabriel Landeskog scored in his second straight game as Colorado continued rolling (3 straight wins; 4 of 5 since the Olympic break).

games.headline: Sharks outgun Canadiens 7-5 in a wild one as Celebrini racks up four points
games.lead: San Jose and Montreal played a full-on track meet, and Macklin Celebrini was at the center of basically everything as the Sharks survived a late Canadiens push.
bullets: Macklin Celebrini posted 1 goal and 3 assists, including his 30th goal of the season, as the Sharks poured in 7 goals and won their third straight.
The game swung hard in the middle: San Jose scored three times in 8:20 in the second to go up 5-2, then Montreal stormed back with three third-period goals to tie it 5-5.
Kiefer Sherwood scored the power-play go-ahead goal with 3:26 left, the late dagger after the Canadiens had erased the deficit.
Montreal finally lost in regulation for the first time since Jan. 24, despite Alex Newhook scoring two power-play goals and Mike Matheson adding three assists.
